reasoning: Pan Zhanle currently holds the long course 100m freestyle world record at 46.40, set at the Paris 2024 Olympics. His record safely survived the 2025 World Aquatics Championships in Singapore, where David Popovici won with 46.51. The odds of this record being broken in the next three months are remarkably remote. First, the 46.40 mark is very strong. Second, Q1 of 2026 does not feature major global long-course championships (like the Olympics or Long Course Worlds) where swimmers typically peak and break sprint world records; most federations hold their long course trials later in the spring. Finally, even though Chinese swimmers have faced intense media scrutiny regarding doping, Pan Zhanle passed numerous rigorous tests in 2024, and any hypothetical future retrospective action by WADA or CAS would involve a protracted legal process that is practically impossible to conclude within a 3-month window. Further, if Pan Zhanle sets a new record himself, he retains the world record holder title, meaning it also works in our favor. Confidence is extremely high.
